title: Quiz: Mixer Hamiltonians
description: Test your understanding of QAOA mixer Hamiltonians

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the mixer Hamiltonian in QAOA?

options.text: To enable transitions between computational basis states

questions.explanation: The mixer Hamiltonian enables exploration by allowing transitions between different computational basis states. Without it, the cost unitary alone would only add phases.

questions.question: What does the XY-mixer preserve that the standard X-mixer does not?

options.text: Number of qubits in state |1⟩ (Hamming weight)

questions.explanation: The XY-mixer swaps |01⟩ ↔ |10⟩ but leaves |00⟩ and |11⟩ unchanged, preserving the total number of 1s (Hamming weight) in the bitstring.

questions.question: What is the ground state of the standard X-mixer B = Σ X_j?
questions.explanation: The ground state of Σ X_j is |+⟩^n, the uniform superposition over all computational basis states. This is why QAOA typically starts from |+⟩^n.

questions.question: A mixer that commutes with the cost Hamiltonian is ideal for QAOA.
questions.explanation: If the mixer commutes with the cost Hamiltonian, the alternating structure of QAOA provides no benefit. Non-commutation is essential for exploration.

questions.question: When should you use the Grover mixer instead of the X-mixer?

options.text: When the problem has hard constraints to satisfy

questions.explanation: The Grover mixer only connects feasible states, making it suitable for constraint satisfaction where the X-mixer might explore infeasible regions.

questions.question: What does the operator (X_i X_j + Y_i Y_j)/2 do in the XY-mixer?

options.text: Swaps the states of qubits i and j when they differ

questions.explanation: The (XX + YY)/2 term swaps |01⟩ ↔ |10⟩ while leaving |00⟩ and |11⟩ unchanged, effectively exchanging the qubit states when they differ.

questions.question: For TSP with one-hot encoding, which mixer type is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: TSP requires preserving row and column constraints in the one-hot encoding. XY-mixers applied to appropriate pairs can swap assignments while maintaining validity.

questions.question: Which are properties of a good mixer for constrained QAOA? (Select all)

options.text: Does not commute with cost Hamiltonian
Can reach all feasible states from initial state

questions.explanation: Good mixers don't commute with cost (a), preserve feasibility (b), and ensure connectivity between feasible states (c). The X operator (d) is common but not required.
